name: Ibiur Reservoir Loop from Ordizia
name_original: Embalse de Ibiur – circular desde Villafranca de Ordizia
description: This moderate 19.6-mile touring cycling route around Embalse de Ibiur offers scenic reflections of Txindoki mountain and charming forests.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ordizia, nestled in the Goierri region of Gipuzkoa, offers diverse terrain and natural landscapes. The area is characterized by the majestic Txindoki Mountain as a backdrop, with the Orio River flowing through the region. Cyclists encounter features such as the Ibiur Reservoir, known for its calm waters, and the Lareo Reservoir, surrounded by beech forests. The routes often pass through picturesque forests and charming villages, providing varied elevation and scenic views suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Ordizia The most popular touring cycling route is Ibiur Reservoir Loop from Ordizia, a 19.6 miles (31.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Ibiur Reservoir, often reflecting Txindoki Mountain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuente de Basasiche – Old Bridge loop from Ordizia, a moderate 23.5 miles (37.8 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including forests and open fields, providing a balanced cycling experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Komuna Public Restroom – Statue of a Worker loop from Ordizia, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. This difficult route explores parts of the region's natural and cultural surroundings. Touring cycling around Ordizia is defined by the presence of Txindoki Mountain, the Ibiur and Lareo Reservoirs, and extensive forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Ordizia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ordizia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ordizia?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Ordizia, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable ride for everyone.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Ordizia offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, there are 2 easy routes available, perfect for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed family outing. These routes often feature gentler terrain and scenic views.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes in Ordizia?
A: Touring cycling routes in Ordizia showcase diverse natural landscapes. You'll often have the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txindoki Mountain&lt;/a&gt; as a backdrop, encounter serene waters like the Ibiur Reservoir, and cycle through picturesque forests, especially around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lareo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; which is surrounded by beautiful beech woods.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its water features. You can cycle around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085617950/ibiur-reservoir-loop-from-ordizia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiur Reservoir Loop from Ordizia&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning reflections of Txindoki Mountain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lareo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3049380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amezketa Stream Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction in the area.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while touring cycling in Ordizia?
A: Ordizia's rich history offers several interesting stops. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/836252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igartza Monumental Complex&lt;/a&gt;, a well-preserved 16th-century site, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4783578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Antigua&lt;/a&gt;. The town's historic center itself is a declared historical-monumental ensemble with ancient buildings.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists, Ordizia offers a good selection of challenging routes. There are 12 difficult routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ormaiztegi Viaduct – View of the Atagoiti Valley loop from Ordizia&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers longer distances.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Ordizia?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Ordizia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085617950/ibiur-reservoir-loop-from-ordizia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiur Reservoir Loop from Ordizia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Basasiche – Old Bridge loop from Ordizia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ordizia?
A: The region offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Autumn is particularly appealing, especially around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lareo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, when the beech forests display vibrant colors. Spring and summer also provide excellent weather for exploring the routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Ordizia?
A: While the Goierri region is generally outdoor-friendly, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's always best to check local signage or with the regional tourist office for the most current information regarding dog policies on specific routes or natural parks.

Q: Are there any routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Ordizia provide excellent viewpoints, often with vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ormaiztegi Viaduct – View of the Atagoiti Valley loop from Ordizia&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is known for its scenic outlooks. The Ibiur Reservoir also offers stunning reflections of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txindoki Mountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Ordizia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ordizia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the reservoirs and forests, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Ordizia?
A: Ordizia is part of the Goierri region, which has public transport options like Euskotren and RENFE. Cyclists should check the specific bike transport policies and schedules of these services, as rules for carrying bicycles can vary by line and time of day.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, starting and ending in Villafranca de Ordizia, generally takes around 2 hours and 5 minutes to complete at a touring cycling pace. The section directly around the Ibiur Reservoir is about 7 km and can take 1.5 to 2.5 hours on its own, depending on your speed and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers varied terrain. You'll encounter forest tracks, which can become muddy, especially after rain, so appropriate gear is recommended. There are also sections on paved roads and some ascending slopes, particularly as you approach areas like Baliarrain. It's a moderate difficulty ride, balancing natural paths with road cycling.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, while classified as moderate, aspects of this route are considered suitable for families. The scenic beauty and varied terrain make it an engaging experience for those with some cycling experience, but beginners might find some sections challenging, especially if muddy.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I see?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to beautiful natural landscapes. The highlight is the Ibiur Reservoir, often called "the mirror of Txindoki" due to the stunning reflections of the Txindoki mountain on its calm waters. The route also winds through charming forests and offers views of picturesque villages like Baliarrain, with its rural charm and historical buildings. The wider Goierri region features mountains, meadows, and scattered farmhouses.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The main attraction is the Ibiur Reservoir itself, known for its reflective waters. You might also pass by an Old Bridge. Additionally, the route can lead you through charming villages like Baliarrain, offering a glimpse into local culture and history with its traditional architecture and the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable in many seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful foliage. Be aware that forest tracks can become muddy during rainy seasons, so checking the weather forecast is advisable, especially in wetter months.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Embalse de Ibiur cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts from Villafranca de Ordizia, you can typically find parking within Villafranca de Ordizia itself. Look for public parking areas in the town to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Ibiur cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's generally a good idea to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near roads, and to ensure you clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions around the reservoir or in villages.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay fees to cycle here?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay fees to cycle the Embalse de Ibiur route. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Donostia - Beasain trail (37%) and the Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior (37%). You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with routes like Aralarko bira and Legorreta-Santamaña-Legorreta.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring a touring bicycle suitable for both paved and unpaved surfaces. Essential items include water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate clothing for the weather, especially rain gear if there's a chance of mud. A camera is also recommended to capture the scenic reflections of Txindoki mountain.
